options: Daily = total for the period; lifetime = per day
Daily = average daily spend; lifetime = total for the period (enables scheduling)
questions.explanation: Daily controls the daily average; lifetime sets the total and enables ad scheduling.
Only with a lifetime budget can delivery hours be defined.

options: Sets the budget per ad set manually
Distributes the budget across ad sets toward the best-performing one
questions.explanation: CBO moves spend dynamically; ABO sets the budget per ad set.

questions.q: Client needs ~600 leads/month, historical CPL ~$4. Base estimate:
questions.explanation: 600 × $4 = $2,400 (estimate, not a guarantee: the cost in Auction fluctuates).

questions.q: Reservation: 1,000,000 people, frequency 3, fixed CPM $4. Budget:
Approximate threshold to exit the learning phase:
options: ~50 optimization events in ~7 days per ad set
questions.explanation: ~50 optimization events in ~7 days stabilize the ad set.

questions.q: Does daily budget spend exactly the same each day?
options: No; it can vary day to day while honoring the period's average
questions.explanation: Meta can spend more on high-opportunity days and offset, keeping the average.

questions.q: A budget too low to gather ~50 events/week causes:
options: Ad set stuck in learning; unstable performance
questions.explanation: Without enough signal the system doesn't optimize well; volatile results.

questions.q: Launch with guaranteed reach and a fixed budget approved by management:
options: Auction with a low Bid Cap
questions.explanation: Guarantee + fixed budget = Reservation with impressions calculation; Reservation doesn't support Sales.

questions.q: What requires a lifetime budget? Select 2.
questions.explanation: Dayparting needs a lifetime budget; Reservation also operates with a period budget.
